children.label: a
children.text: When the terms “employer”, “employee”, and “wage” are used in this chapter in relation to the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938, as amended [ et seq.], they shall have the same meaning as when used in such Act of 1938.29 U.S.C. 201

children.label: b
children.text: When the term “employer” is used in this chapter in relation to the Walsh-Healey Act or Bacon-Davis Act it shall mean the contractor or subcontractor covered by such Act.1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: c
children.text: When the term “employee” is used in this chapter in relation to the Walsh-Healey Act or the Bacon-Davis Act it shall mean any individual employed by the contractor or subcontractor covered by such Act in the performance of his contract or subcontract.1

children.label: d
children.text: The term “Wash-Healey Act” means the Act entitled “An Act to provide conditions for the purchase of supplies and the making of contracts by the United States, and for other purposes”, approved (), as amended; and the term “Bacon-Davis Act” means the Act entitled “An Act to amend the Act approved , relating to the rate of wages for laborers and mechanics employed by contractors and subcontractors on public buildings”, approved (), as amended.249 Stat. 203649 Stat. 1011So in original. Probably should be “Walsh-Healey Act”.2June 30, 1936March 3, 1931August 30, 193511

children.label: e
children.text: As used in the term “State” means any State of the United States or the District of Columbia or any Territory or possession of the United States.section 255 of this title
